warnings: Owner for action items is not specified
Open-ended questions may require additional human reading when the document has contextual ambiguity.

decision_summary.label: Renegotiate
decision_summary.summary: Master Services Agreement - Vendor Draft: 4 risk(s), 1 gap(s) and 4 mitigation action(s) identified. Top risk: Delayed service due to security incidents Main evidence gap: Owner for action items is not specified
decision_summary.due_date: within 10 business days after determining that a material incident occurred

next_steps: Ensure security incident notifications are prompt and clear

top_blockers.title: Delayed service due to security incidents
top_blockers.recommendation: Ensure prompt and clear notifications are established for security incidents.

business_impact.label: Impact 1
business_impact.detail: Potential delays in service delivery and operational inefficiencies

business_impact.label: Impact 2
business_impact.detail: Lack of oversight could lead to compliance issues and reputational risks

findings.title: Delayed service due to security incidents
findings.description: The agreement does not specify a clear timeline for notifying the customer of material security incidents, which could lead to delays in service delivery.
findings.recommendation: Ensure prompt and clear notifications are established for security incidents.
findings.snippet: Provider will notify Customer within 10 business days after determining that a material incident occurred.

findings.title: No customer audit rights
findings.description: The agreement lacks provisions for customer audits, which could result in a lack of oversight and accountability.
findings.recommendation: Implement clear guidelines for customer audits.
findings.snippet: No customer audit right. Provider may instead share a summary letter or certification at its discretion.

findings.title: Unapproved updates to subprocessor list
findings.description: The agreement allows the provider to update its subprocessor list without approval, which could introduce security risks and lack of transparency.
findings.recommendation: Establish clear guidelines for subprocessor updates.
findings.snippet: Provider may update its subprocessor list from time to time without approval; notice is provided only upon request.

findings.title: No stated data deletion deadline
findings.description: The agreement does not specify a timeline for deleting customer data after termination, which could result in data not being deleted in a timely manner.
findings.snippet: No commercially reasonable efforts to delete Customer Data following termination. No deletion deadline is stated.

evidence_trail.title: Delayed service due to security incidents
evidence_trail.snippet: Provider will notify Customer within 10 business days after determining that a material incident occurred.

evidence_trail.title: No customer audit rights
evidence_trail.snippet: No customer audit right. Provider may instead share a summary letter or certification at its discretion.

evidence_trail.title: Unapproved updates to subprocessor list
evidence_trail.snippet: Provider may update its subprocessor list from time to time without approval; notice is provided only upon request.

evidence_trail.title: No stated data deletion deadline
evidence_trail.snippet: No commercially reasonable efforts to delete Customer Data following termination. No deletion deadline is stated.

runs.summary: Master Services Agreement - Vendor Draft: 5 risk(s), 1 gap(s) and 2 mitigation action(s) identified. Top risk: Delayed service due to provider's discretion in notifying security incidents Main evidence gap: Owner for action items not specified
warnings: Owner for action items not specified
Open-ended questions may require additional human reading when the document has contextual ambiguity.

decision_summary.label: Renegotiate
decision_summary.summary: Multiple high-risk gaps in security notification, audit rights, data deletion, and liability caps require renegotiation before approval.
decision_summary.due_date: within 10 business days after determining that a material incident occurred

watchouts: Owner for action items not specified
Open-ended questions may require additional human reading when the document has contextual ambiguity.
next_steps: Ensure provider updates subprocessor list with approval
Develop a comprehensive data deletion policy
Negotiate shorter notification timelines or defined escalation procedures for security incidents.
Establish formal audit rights and define specific data deletion timelines and verification methods.

top_blockers.title: Owner for action items not specified
No defined timeline for security incident notification
Lack of customer audit rights and limited data deletion obligations
top_blockers.recommendation: Establish formal audit rights and define specific data deletion timelines and verification methods.

top_blockers.title: Unbounded liability exposure due to fee-based cap

business_impact.label: Impact 1
business_impact.detail: Increased risk of regulatory penalties due to weak data governance

business_impact.label: Impact 2
business_impact.detail: Potential service disruption from delayed security incident response

business_impact.label: Impact 3
business_impact.detail: Limited recourse in case of data breach or misuse

findings.title: Delayed service due to provider's discretion in notifying security incidents
findings.description: Provider will notify Customer within 10 business days after determining that a material incident occurred, which may cause service delays.
findings.recommendation: Negotiate shorter notification timelines or defined escalation procedures for security incidents.
findings.snippet: Provider will notify Customer within 10 business days after determining that a material incident occurred.

findings.title: Lack of customer audit rights and limited data deletion obligations
findings.description: No customer audit right; provider may share a summary letter or certification at its discretion and use commercially reasonable efforts to delete customer data following termination.
findings.recommendation: Establish formal audit rights and define specific data deletion timelines and verification methods.
findings.snippet: No customer audit right. Provider may instead share a summary letter or certification at its discretion. Provider will use commercially reasonable efforts to delete Customer Data following termination.

findings.title: Unspecified deletion deadline for customer data
findings.description: No deletion deadline is stated for customer data following termination, creating uncertainty in data management.
findings.recommendation: Define a specific timeline for data deletion post-termination and include verification requirements.
findings.snippet: No commercially reasonable efforts to delete Customer Data following termination. No deletion deadline is stated.

findings.title: Use of de-identified service data for product development without customer consent
findings.description: Provider may use de-identified service data for product development and model improvement without explicit customer consent.
findings.recommendation: Require opt-in consent or provide an opt-out mechanism for use of de-identified data in product development.
findings.snippet: Provider may use de-identified service data for product development and model improvement.

findings.title: Aggregate liability cap limited to fees paid in the prior 12 months
findings.description: Aggregate liability limited to fees paid in prior 12 months, including for confidentiality and data-security claims, offering limited financial protection.
findings.recommendation: Negotiate a higher liability cap or carve-outs for data breaches and security failures.
findings.snippet: Aggregate liability limited to fees paid in prior 12 months, including for confidentiality and data-security claims.

evidence_trail.title: Delayed service due to provider's discretion in notifying security incidents
evidence_trail.snippet: Provider will notify Customer within 10 business days after determining that a material incident occurred.

evidence_trail.title: Lack of customer audit rights and limited data deletion obligations
evidence_trail.snippet: No customer audit right. Provider may instead share a summary letter or certification at its discretion. Provider will use commercially reasonable efforts to delete Customer Data following termination.

evidence_trail.title: Unspecified deletion deadline for customer data
evidence_trail.snippet: No commercially reasonable efforts to delete Customer Data following termination. No deletion deadline is stated.

evidence_trail.title: Use of de-identified service data for product development without customer consent
evidence_trail.snippet: Provider may use de-identified service data for product development and model improvement.

evidence_trail.title: Aggregate liability cap limited to fees paid in the prior 12 months
evidence_trail.snippet: Aggregate liability limited to fees paid in prior 12 months, including for confidentiality and data-security claims.
